data.title: Vertiv Stock (VRT) Dip Opens Door for Thrifty Options Traders
data.description:
In the near term, Vertiv Holdings (VRT) appears to be a speculative bet. On paper, the company checks all the right boxes: it designs, manufactures, and services mission-critical digital infrastructure for data centers and communications networks. With artificial intelligence accelerating, VRT might seem like an obvious winner. Yet growing concerns over a potential AI bubble have cast a shadow across the sector, weighing on investor sentiment.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That caution is already showing up in the price action. VRT shares are down roughly 4% to start the year and have fallen more than 8% over the past month. Even so, it would be a mistake to ignore the broader trend. Institutional capital continues to flow aggressively into machine learning and AI-driven infrastructure, and there's little evidence suggesting that momentum is poised to fade anytime soon.
data.author: Joshua Enomoto

data.title: Goldman Thinks Microsoft Stock (MSFT) Is Being Underestimated
data.description:
Top Goldman analyst Gabriela Borges initiated coverage on Microsoft (MSFT) with a Buy rating and a $655 price target, implying about 37% upside from current levels. Borges said investors' views on Microsoft's AI outlook have grown more cautious recently, but she believes those worries are overdone. In her view, Microsoft is better placed than most large tech firms as AI use grows across businesses and consumers.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Why Goldman Is Bullish on Microsoft

The 4-star analyst pointed to Microsoft's reach across the AI stack, from cloud services to AI tools built into everyday products like Office and Windows. That wide presence gives the company more ways to grow as AI adoption picks up.

She also highlighted Microsoft's Copilot tools and AI agents, which are being rolled out across its products. She said this makes AI easier for customers to use and supports steady demand over time.

Because Microsoft controls both the platform and the software, Borges expects the company to deliver more stable growth than rivals that rely on a single product or partner. Looking ahead, Goldman expects that Microsoft could earn more than $35 per share by fiscal 2030 in a stronger scenario. That would mark faster earnings growth than most other large tech companies. 

Is Microsoft Stock a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 32 Buys and two Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 15% rally in its share price over the past year, the average Microsoft price target of $631.36 per share implies 31.73% upside risk.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Stock Market News Review: SPY, QQQ Shrug Off Powell's DOJ Investigation ahead of Key Inflation Data
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) reversed early-morning losses driven by the Department of Justice's investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell over the central bank's $2.5 billion renovation of its Washington, D.C., headquarters. In addition, SPY was able to secure a new intraday record high of $696.09 toward the end of the trading session.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In a video statement, Powell pushed back against the investigation and said that the threat of criminal charges stems from the Fed setting interest rates independently and based on economic data rather than following President Trump's views. Trump has repeatedly called on the central bank to lower rates while leveling criticism against Powell for delaying rate cuts.

Trump has denied any role in the investigation. "I don't know anything about it, but he's certainly not very good at the Fed, and he's not very good at building buildings," he told NBC News.

The investigation could lead to "highly negative consequences," according to a joint statement released by a group of economists that includes every single living former Fed Chair and several former Treasury Secretaries. The consequences, which include a loss of Fed independence and the risk of rising inflation, mirror previous scenarios seen in emerging markets.

Furthermore, JPMorgan warned that risks to the Fed's autonomy could result in "near-term underperformance" for U.S. stocks. At the same time, a buying opportunity could emerge if the situation de-escalates amid a resilient economic backdrop and strong corporate fundamentals, said Andrew Tyler, JPMorgan's Global Head of Market Intelligence.

Meanwhile, Trump warned that the U.S. could take a trillion-dollar hit if the Supreme Court rules to overturn his tariffs. "In other words, if the Supreme Court rules against the United States of America on this National Security bonanza, WE'RE SCREWED!" he said in a Truth Social post. The Supreme Court could announce a decision in the case as early as Wednesday. Last week, National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett said that the Trump administration has already begun looking into legal alternatives to impose tariffs if SCOTUS strikes down the duties.

Looking ahead, the Bureau of Labor Statistics will release December's inflation data on Tuesday. Analysts expect the Consumer Price Index (CPI) to rise by 0.3% month-over-month and 2.7% year-over-year. Core CPI, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, is expected to increase by 0.3% over the prior month and 2.7% over the past year as well.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.16% gain, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) returned 0.08%.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Donald Trump's $1.5T Defense Budget Reframes the Case for Northrop Grumman Stock (NOC)
data.description:
Northrop Grumman (NOC) is not usually the kind of stock that moves on a single number. Yet President Trump's talk of a $1.5 trillion U.S. defence budget for 2027 has done exactly that, forcing Wall Street to reassess the scale and durability of the current rearmament cycle. Against that backdrop, the Pentagon's already vast $901 billion budget for 2026 stops looking generous and starts to look merely provisional.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For aerospace and defence investors, the key issue is less the politics than the path dependency. Is this campaign rhetoric that fades during the appropriations process, or an early signal of a more overtly "war-like" economic posture—one in which defence primes function both as an earnings compounder and a portfolio hedge?

On that framing, Northrop Grumman stands out. Since the start of 2026, the stock is up a solid 8.5%, with Trump's defence rhetoric effectively reinforcing its position as a prime beneficiary of an increasingly muscular, almost "Age of Empires" style of geopolitics now playing out across multiple theatres.
data.author: Nikolaos Sismanis

data.title: U.S. Futures Edge Lower Ahead of CPI Data and Major Bank Earnings
data.description:
U.S. stock futures edged lower Monday night as investors grew cautious ahead of key consumer inflation data and the first major bank earnings of the season. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were down 0.31%, 0.14%, and 0.2%, respectively, at 7:53 p.m. EDT on January 12.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In yesterday's trading session, the S&P 500 closed with a 0.16% gain, while the Dow and the Nasdaq Composite rose 0.17% and 0.08%.

Looking ahead, investors are focused on Tuesday's consumer price index (CPI) report, which is expected to give a clearer view of inflation after data gaps caused by last fall's long U.S. government shutdown.

At the same time, earnings season has started. Investors are watching for JPMorgan's (JPM) fourth-quarter earnings, due before the market opens on Tuesday. The report will kick off a wave of results from other major banks, including Bank of America, Citigroup, and Morgan Stanley, in the days ahead.

Together, these reports should give investors an early look at consumer spending, travel demand, trading activity, and deal flow as the year gets underway.

data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Why Envirotech Vehicles (EVTV) Stock Skyrocketed 442% Today and What Comes Next.
data.description:
Can you feel the ground moving beneath your feet? Shares of Envirotech Vehicles (NASDAQ:EVTV) skyrocketed 442% today after investors reacted to a major announcement involving its merger partner, AZIO AI.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

AZIO AI disclosed that it has received a binding purchase order for 256 Nvidia B300 GPUs, translating into approximately $107 million in contractual revenue, with a 30% deposit expected in the coming weeks. Importantly, the order is part of a broader government-related pipeline that could reach up to $200 million across Southeast Asia and other regions.

But the real market reaction today was about potential future revenue and strategic pivoting rather than EVTV's historical performance as a maker of electric vehicles and heavy-lift drones. It's one thing to talk about pivoting into AI infrastructure; it's another to see real world orders from institutional and potentially government customers – and that's what drove investors to jump back in.

Before today, EVTV's chart has been brutal. The stock has fallen sharply over the past year, reflecting weak financials, negative gross profit, shrinking equity, widening losses, and persistent negative operating and free cash flow, according to the TipRanks AI report.

That TipRanks AI model currently rates EVTV Neutral with a score of 41 and a price target of just $0.55, highlighting that the business on its own hasn't shown strong fundamental support. As TipRanks puts it, the score "is held down primarily by weak financial fundamentals – negative gross profit, widening losses, shrinking equity with higher leverage, and continued negative operating/free cash flow."

So while today's headline percentage move feels explosive, it's important to remember that no traditional Wall Street analysts are actively covering EVTV – meaning this rally is being driven largely by retail interest and speculative positioning around the AI merger narrative rather than deep institutional conviction. The only current coverage we have is from TipRanks' AI-based model, which provides a somewhat cautious backdrop and underscores why investors should be thoughtful about today's price action.

However, even within that Neutral view, there are positive factors cited that could justify renewed enthusiasm if executed well. TipRanks notes an ~83% trailing-twelve-month revenue rebound, suggesting durable demand traction in commercial markets and a foundation for longer-term margin recovery. It also highlights the company's commercial-focused model with recurring services and a planned acquisition of AZIO AI to add software/AI capabilities that could create recurring software revenue streams beyond hardware alone.

Looking ahead, what happens next for EVTV will likely hinge on three big milestones:

  • Progress toward a definitive merger agreement and closing with AZIO AI, including regulatory and shareholder approvals.
  • Execution and delivery of the current GPU purchase order and further government pipeline contracts.
  • Whether future communications reflect real execution rather than just strategic vision.

If those check boxes start converting into tangible revenue and operating improvement, today's move may look prescient. If not, the Neutral rating and the lack of deep analyst support could mean volatility ahead.
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: 'The Gap Is Still Wide,' Says Top Analyst about Nvidia Stock
data.description:
This article was written by Shalu Saraf and reviewed by Gilan Miller-Gertz.

Top Baird analyst Tristan Gerra sees Nvidia (NVDA) maintaining a clear lead in artificial intelligence, based on the latest industry checks. In a recent note, Gerra, a five-star analyst, reiterated his Outperform rating on the stock and kept a $275 price target, again naming Nvidia a "top idea for 2026."

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

It is worth noting that Gerra ranks 385 out of more than 10,000 analysts tracked by TipRanks. He has a success rate of 56%, with an average return per rating of 16.20% over a one-year timeframe.

Will Nvidia Keep its AI lead?

Gerra said Nvidia's edge goes well beyond selling chips. He believes the company is building full AI ecosystems that bring together hardware, software, and tools on one platform. As AI systems grow more complex, he said this makes it more likely for customers to stay with Nvidia rather than switch to newer or less proven options.

The analyst also highlighted Nvidia's massive developer base as a key advantage. Around seven million developers are now building on Nvidia's platforms, which Gerra said raises switching costs and creates a strong barrier for rivals trying to gain share.

Some investors worry that competition could rise as AI inference becomes a larger part of demand over time. Gerra pushed back on that view, noting that many in the industry expect Nvidia to retain a far-leading share in AI even as inference grows much larger starting in 2028. In his view, Nvidia's platform depth puts it in a strong position across both training and inference.

Also, Gerra said industry checks and insights from Nvidia's recent CES presentation support his outlook. He believes Nvidia's early lead, combined with its broad ecosystem, continues to give the company a clear advantage as AI adoption expands.

Is Nvidia a Buy or Sell Stock?  

According to TipRanks, NVDA stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 39 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell assigned in the last three months. At $264.97, the Nvidia average share price target implies almost 43% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Trump Administration Edges Closer to Taiwan Tariff Deal
data.description:
The Trump administration is advancing toward the final stages of a trade deal with Taiwan that would see the island's tariff rate reduced to 15% from 20% and Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) agreeing to build at least five semiconductor fabs in Arizona, according to The New York Times. A framework of the deal could be announced as soon as this month.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Taiwan Semiconductor already has one semiconductor fab running in Arizona, with a second facility expected to be completed in 2028.

U.S. Weighs 15% Tariff for Taiwan, Matching Asian Allies

The proposed 15% tariff rate on Taiwan's imports matches the duties negotiated with Japan and South Korea, while China's rate stands at roughly 30%. Taiwan Semiconductor's investment in the U.S. is a key part of the deal as the Trump administration seeks to bolster domestic chipmaking.

In 2024, the U.S. exported $42.5 billion of goods to Taiwan and imported $116.3 billion of goods from it, resulting in a U.S. trade deficit of $73.7 billion.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: 'Time to Hop In,' Says Investor About AMD Stock
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) shares have not been immune to mounting fears of an AI bubble, which have weighed on many of the market's former high-flyers after a strong stretch through much of 2025. After hitting an all-time high in October, AMD shares are now down about 21%.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In fact, much of that decline has unfolded since the company's Q3 2025 earnings report, a move that appears somewhat surprising given that the company's underlying performance has remained strong.

According to one 5-star investor, known by the pseudonym RI Research, the recent weakness in AMD's share price reflects sentiment rather than substance. In the investor's view, AMD's fundamentals "remain rock-solid, while there are also accelerating AI-related tailwinds."

The numbers back up that confidence. AMD topped expectations on both the top and bottom lines in Q3, posting a 5.6% revenue beat alongside a 2.5% upside surprise on earnings. Revenue growth accelerated to 35.6% year over year, improving meaningfully from the 31.7% pace reported in Q2. Moreover, despite growing capex spending, the company's net cash position improved by some $1.5 billion in the latest quarter.

AMD is one of the biggest beneficiaries of the global AI data center spending. This trend appears primed to continue, declares RI, as the largest technology companies are all increasing their AI capex in 2026.

Moreover, these spending projects are neither one-off nor short-term, states the investor. The long-term planning required gives a "picks and shovels" company like AMD strong revenue and EPS visibility going forward.

Don't just take RI's word for it, either. The investor namechecks a couple of Wall Street pros who have made AMD one of their top picks for 2026.

Though competition is the "major risk" facing AMD, and Nvidia remains the undisputed data center leader, AMD's FY2028 forward price-to-earnings ratio below 15x is just too compelling for the investor.

So, for RI, the verdict is crystal clear: "AMD is dirt cheap at the moment." On that basis, the investor rates AMD shares a Strong Buy. (To watch RI Research's track record, click here)

Wall Street isn't pushing back on that view. With 28 Buy recommendations versus 9 Holds, AMD earns a Moderate Buy consensus rating. The Street's 12-month average price target of $283.34 suggests there's ~36% upside on the table from here. (See AMD stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: 'Don't Bet On It,' Says Top Investor About Tesla Stock
data.description:
On one level, the optimism around Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) stock is pretty easy to understand. After all, the company's big bets on autonomous driving and humanoid robots are rooted in the pursuit of potentially trillions of dollars in future opportunities.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In that light, drooping EV sales in the here and now haven't raised too many alarm bells. TSLA's share price continues to enjoy high valuation premiums – ones that are head and shoulders above those of other automobile manufacturers – even after two straight years of falling deliveries.

Tesla's latest Q4 deliveries update is a prime example of this phenomenon. Deliveries slid 15.6% year-over-year to 418,227 vehicles, yet the stock remains comfortably above $440 – a sign that investors are still willing to look past today's delivery slowdown and bet that Elon Musk's long-term autonomy vision will ultimately outweigh current fundamentals.

However, top investor Daniel Sparks isn't so confident such a lucrative future awaits the company and its investors.

"What if Tesla's Robotaxi service turns out to be a costly and commoditized business?" asks the 5-star investor, who is among the top 1% of stock pros covered by TipRanks.

Sparks lays out two main concerns. The first revolves around Tesla's capital expenditures, which were close to $9 billion in 2025. The investor cites recent statements from the company's CFO, who acknowledged that Tesla's capex is expected to "increase substantially" in 2026. Sparks worries that these costs could balloon even higher than investors are expecting.

The second issue the investor raises is the profitability of the future robotaxis, which Sparks points out is going to be competing in "a highly competitive, price-sensitive market." He notes that large tech giants (Alphabet and Amazon), EV makers (Rivian, Lucid, and BYD), and existing ride-sharing companies (Uber and Lyft) are all racing to offer their own autonomous driving services.

In other words, rather than pushing the boundaries of technological advancements, the full self-driving market could find itself turning into something resembling a commodity. Viewed in that light, the margins might not be all that attractive.

"Intuitively, a taxi service boils down to price when customers select their rides. So, it may be difficult for one company to differentiate itself on any factor other than price," adds Sparks.

Ultimately, that doesn't leave enough margin for Sparks to feel comfortable with an investment in Tesla.

"I believe the bull case for Robotaxi is largely priced in, and the bear case is mostly ignored," concludes Sparks. (To watch Sparks' track record, click here)

Wall Street's stance reflects that tension. While 13 analysts are willing to give Elon Musk the benefit of the doubt, 10 remain on the sidelines and 7 recommend selling, resulting in a consensus Hold (i.e., Neutral) rating. Meanwhile, the Street's 12-month average price target of $405.94 implies a downside of high single digits from current levels. (See TSLA stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross
